title: Giving directions in writing
description: Write clear directions in emails and messages using imperative verbs, prepositions of movement, and landmark language.

content.title: Imperatives for directions
content.body:
When giving written directions, use IMPERATIVE verbs â€” the base form of the verb.

Basic direction verbs:
â€¢ Turn left / Turn right
â€¢ Go straight ahead / Continue straight
â€¢ Take the first / second / third turning
â€¢ Cross the road / cross at the signal
â€¢ Go past the temple / go past the petrol station
â€¢ Walk along the main road
â€¢ Go through the gate / through the underpass
â€¢ Head towards the flyover
â€¢ Keep going until you reach...
â€¢ You will see... / You can't miss it!

Distance and time:
â€¢ It's about 200 metres / about 5 minutes on foot.
content.grammar_note:
Imperatives use base verb â€” no subject needed
'Turn left' NOT 'You should turn left' (though both are possible)
Written directions use numbers: 'Take the 2nd right'

content.title: Prepositions of movement
content.body:
These prepositions describe how you move through space:

â€¢ ALONG â€” move in the same direction as a road
  'Walk along MG Road for 200 metres.'

â€¢ PAST â€” go further than a landmark without stopping
  'Go past the Apollo hospital.'

â€¢ THROUGH â€” move from one side to the other
  'Go through the main gate.'

â€¢ ACROSS â€” cross from one side to the other
  'Walk across the bridge.'

â€¢ TOWARDS â€” in the direction of something
  'Head towards the railway station.'

â€¢ INTO â€” enter a place
  'Turn into the lane on your left.'
content.grammar_note:
along = direction
past = movement beyond a point
through = from side to side
across = from one side to the other

vocabulary.meaning: a well-known place used as a reference point
vocabulary.example: The temple is a useful landmark.

vocabulary.meaning: where two or more roads meet
a bridge that goes over a road
vocabulary.example: Go under the flyover and take a right.
At the roundabout, take the third exit.
The parking is adjacent to the building.

sentences: Walk along the main road for about 300 metres.
Turn left at the traffic signal near the petrol station.
Go past the bus stand and take the second right.
Go through the gate â€” the office is on the ground floor.
You can't miss it â€” it's a large blue building on the corner.

content.question: Which preposition correctly completes: 'Walk ___ the bridge and turn right.'?
content.explanation: ACROSS = move from one side to the other. A bridge goes across water â€” you walk 'across' it.

content.question: Which sentence gives the clearest written direction?
options: It's somewhere near a temple, I think.
Take the second left after the petrol station â€” the building is on your right, opposite the post office.
You'll find it if you look.
Near the temple, go somewhere and then right.
content.correct: Take the second left after the petrol station â€” the building is on your right, opposite the post office.
content.explanation: Good written directions: specific landmarks + numbered turns + final location.

content.question: Complete: 'Go ___ the main road until you reach the railway station.'
content.explanation: ALONG = travel in the same direction as a road. You go 'along' a road.

content.question: What does 'You can't miss it!' mean in directions?
options: The place is very far away.
The place is hidden and hard to find.
The place is very obvious and easy to spot.
content.correct: The place is very obvious and easy to spot.
content.explanation: 'You can't miss it!' = it's very visible and easy to find â€” a common reassurance in directions.

content.instruction: Fill in the correct preposition of movement.

sentences.text: Walk ___ the bridge to get to the other side of the river.
Go ___ the school â€” don't stop â€” keep walking till the next signal.
Head ___ the hospital and then turn right.
Walk ___ Anna Salai for about 500 metres.
Go ___ the main gate and the reception is straight ahead.

content.instruction: Complete these written directions to a restaurant.

sentences.text: From the metro station, ___ (exit/walk) through the north exit.
Go ___ the petrol station â€” don't turn there.
___ along that road for about 200 metres.
The restaurant is on the ___ side, opposite a green park.

content.answer: Take the second right at the signal.
Go past the hospital and then take a left.

content.instruction: Match the direction instruction to the preposition of movement.

pairs.left: Move in the same direction as the road
Go further than the temple without stopping
Enter from one side and exit the other
Move from one side to the other (bridge/road)
Face in the direction of something

content.title: Sending directions in a message â€” Chennai
content.setting: Priya is hosting a housewarming party. Her friend Ankit from out of town asks for directions from Chennai Central station to her flat.

dialogue.speaker: Ankit
dialogue.text: Priya! So excited about the housewarming. Can you send me directions from Chennai Central? I'll be coming by auto.

dialogue.speaker: Priya
dialogue.text: Of course! So from Chennai Central, tell the auto driver to go towards Anna Salai â€” that's the main road.

dialogue.speaker: Priya
dialogue.text: Go along Anna Salai past the LIC building â€” it's a big tall building, you can't miss it. Keep going for about a kilometre.

dialogue.speaker: Priya
dialogue.text: After that, you'll see a big signal at the junction with a petrol station on the left. Turn left there.

dialogue.speaker: Ankit
dialogue.text: Turn left at the petrol station signal. Okay!

dialogue.speaker: Priya
dialogue.text: Then go straight along that road â€” go past a small temple on the right, then cross the small bridge over the canal.

dialogue.speaker: Ankit
dialogue.text: Temple on the right, then across the bridge.

dialogue.speaker: Priya
dialogue.text: After the bridge, take the first right â€” it's a narrow lane. Go through the gate at the end â€” our building is called 'Sai Krupa Apartments'.

dialogue.speaker: Ankit
dialogue.text: Sai Krupa Apartments â€” got it. What floor?

dialogue.speaker: Priya
dialogue.text: Third floor, flat 3B. The lift is right opposite the entrance. Total distance from the station is about 4 kilometres â€” maybe 15 minutes by auto.

dialogue.speaker: Ankit
dialogue.text: Perfect! Should I save your number in case I get lost?

dialogue.speaker: Priya
dialogue.text: Yes! And I'll send you the Google Maps pin too â€” just in case. See you Saturday!

comprehension_questions.question: What road does Priya tell Ankit to go towards first?
Where does Ankit turn left?
options: At the signal near the petrol station
comprehension_questions.correct: At the signal near the petrol station

comprehension_questions.question: What is the name of Priya's building?

content.instruction: Find and correct the mistake in the directions.

sentences.wrong: Walk across the main road for 500 metres.
sentences.correct: Walk along the main road for 500 metres.
sentences.explanation: ALONG = travel in the same direction as a road. ACROSS = from one side to the other.

sentences.wrong: Go past through the gate â€” the office is inside.
sentences.correct: Go through the gate â€” the office is inside.
sentences.explanation: PAST and THROUGH are not used together here. 'Go through the gate' means enter it.

sentences.wrong: Towards head the station and turn left.
sentences.correct: Head towards the station and turn left.
sentences.explanation: Correct word order: verb + preposition + object. 'Head towards' (not 'towards head').

content.scenario: A foreign colleague emails you asking how to reach your Bangalore office from MG Road metro station. Which written direction is clearest?
options: Just come towards our area, it's near a temple.

Exit MG Road metro at Gate 3. Walk along Brigade Road for about 400 metres, go past the CafÃ© Coffee Day on the left, and turn right at the junction. The building is the third on the left â€” a glass-fronted building called 'Tech Hub'. It's about a 7-minute walk.
It's not far from MG Road.
Take a left somewhere and keep going.
content.correct: Exit MG Road metro at Gate 3. Walk along Brigade Road for about 400 metres, go past the CafÃ© Coffee Day on the left, and turn right at the junction. The building is the third on the left â€” a glass-fronted building called 'Tech Hub'. It's about a 7-minute walk.
content.explanation: Good written directions: specific exit, road name, landmark, turn, building description, and estimated time.

content.instruction: Give written-style directions from a well-known location to your home, college, or office. Use landmarks, imperative verbs, and prepositions of movement.
content.prompt: Give directions from a local landmark to a place you know well. Use at least 6 direction steps.
content.example_response: Here are directions from Besant Nagar beach to my flat in Chennai. Exit the beach from the main entrance and head towards the Elliot's Beach road. Walk along the beach road going north for about 300 metres. Go past the popular Ratna CafÃ© on your right â€” don't turn there. Continue straight until you reach a signal with a petrol station on the left corner. Turn left at that signal. Walk along that road for about 150 metres and go past a small park. After the park, take the first right â€” it's a quiet lane. Go through the white metal gate at the end of the lane. My building, Sunrise Apartments, is straight ahead â€” it's a four-storey pink building, you can't miss it. I'm on the second floor, Flat 2A. The whole walk takes about 10 minutes from the beach.

content.instruction: A friend is visiting your city for the first time. They are at the main railway station. Give them step-by-step written directions to a famous local restaurant or attraction you like.
content.prompt: Give detailed directions from the main railway station to a local restaurant or attraction. Speak as if writing a message.
content.example_response: Hey! So glad you're finally in Chennai! Here are the directions from Chennai Central to Saravana Bhavan on Usman Road â€” it's one of the best places for South Indian food! From Chennai Central station, take the main exit and head towards the Anna Salai road â€” that's the wide main road facing you. Walk along Anna Salai going south for about 600 metres. You'll pass a big LIC building on your right â€” keep going. At the big roundabout, take the second exit â€” that puts you on the road towards T. Nagar. Go along that road past the Panagal Park bus stop and continue until you see a big intersection. Turn right at that intersection â€” you're now on Usman Road. Walk along Usman Road for about 200 metres. Saravana Bhavan is on the left side â€” it's a large restaurant with green and white signage, you absolutely can't miss it. Total walking time from the station: about 20 minutes. Or just take an auto and show the driver 'Saravana Bhavan, Usman Road, T. Nagar' â€” everyone knows it!

content.instruction: Complete this WhatsApp-style direction message using prepositions and direction verbs.
content.text:
Hey Ravi! Here's how to get to our office from Silk Board junction:

1. ___ (1) towards the flyover â€” don't go under it, take the service road on the left.
2. Walk ___ (2) the service road for about 400 metres.
3. ___ (3) the petrol station â€” keep going.
4. At the next signal, ___ (4) right.
5. ___ (5) along that road past a school on your left.
6. ___ (6) through the blue iron gate â€” the guard will let you in.
7. Our building is ___ (7) the car park â€” 'Brightworks Tower', 5th floor.

Total time: ___ (8) 12 minutes on foot. You can't ___ (9) it â€” it's a tall glass building. Let me know when you're close and I'll come down!

content.title: Lesson 11 complete â€” Giving directions in writing
key_points: Use IMPERATIVE verbs for directions: Turn, Go, Walk, Take, Head
ALONG = travel the length of a road
PAST = move further than a landmark
THROUGH = from one side to the other (gates, tunnels)
ACROSS = from one side to the other (bridges, roads)
TOWARDS = in the direction of something
Use landmarks + numbered turns + distance/time estimates for clear directions
